name: fierce
purpose: Active DNS brute-force with zone transfer attempts, wildcard detection, and IP range reverse-DNS sweep
author: Robert 'RSnake' Hansen (original Perl); mschwager (Python rewrite)
why_notable: Robert 'RSnake' Hansen wrote fierce.pl in 2006 as the first tool to combine DNS AXFR zone transfer attempts with active brute-force in a single pass. RSnake is one of the most influential web security researchers of the 2000s — he ran ha.ckers.org, the pre-social-media hub for web security discussion, and co-coined clickjacking and wrote Slowloris. fierce's wide scan feature (PTR reverse-lookups across the IP range of discovered hosts) finds services that passive tools miss entirely: internal subdomains created yesterday, staging environments, services with no TLS certificates. mschwager rewrote it cleanly in Python 3 around 2017–2019.
